what_it_does: Proposes a generative UX layer for rendering concurrent-cognition swarms as glanceable, animated, altitude-translated shapes (Pulse, River, Nodes) and defines live steering gestures (boost/mute, cast override, reply provenance) plus the RHM reading its own cognition as a combined voice-and-canvas interaction.
observations: The file is explicitly generative / design-surface status: open, not built, intended to be decided with Tim.
It reframes the swarm not as N discrete role cards but as a river/terrain whose shape encodes magnitude, convergence, failures, and contributions.
It defines three nested altitudes: Pulse (default ambient glyph), River (converging flow), Nodes (literal role cards).
It maps cognition data to perceptual encodings: dilation/width for thinking depth, worn/faint tributaries for contribution, dry gaps for stalled roles, red silted stubs for failures.
Motion is treated as content: real-time cascade fill, reply parts lighting as spoken, ambient 'already knew' ripple, replay via durable event log.
Steering primitives: boost/mute a role (slot-weight manipulation), per-turn cast override, and 'why did this part say that' provenance back-lighting.
The RHM reading its own cognition is framed as a single substrate producing both spoken explanation and canvas lighting.
It lists open forks and hard dependencies, especially the weighted slot-scheduler for boost/mute.
It cites many internal sources, code locations, and AGENTS.md rules with epistemic tags (Observed/Designed/Open).
standouts: The 'shape-not-count' rule: default view must never be 30 nodes; the swarm collapses into a single continuous glyph.
The steering metaphor collapses with mechanism: widening a tributary literally increases slot budget because budget = attention.
The 'why did this part say that' affordance uses actual injection edges as the explanation, avoiding generated confabulation.
The view/voice unity: one run://<turn> read drives both RHM speech and canvas lighting.
conventions: Uses epistemic tags (Observed/Designed/Open) and explicit reuse/net-new labels.
Honours addressing constraint run:// and rejects swarm:// / cog:// without confirmation.
References internal files via [[...]] and code line ranges.
Follows altitude-transformation principle: default up-translated, drill-down for detail.
Includes AGENTS.md rule citations (one source, fail loud, navigable visual surface).
Generative design doc format with status, one-sentence summary, sections A-I, sources.
concerns: Heavy generative/speculative content may be mistaken for implementation plan; many items are Designed/Open and depend on unbuilt backend (weighted slot-scheduler).
Aesthetic decisions are repeatedly deferred to 'needs-tim', leaving the concrete form unspecified.
Some proposed mappings (e.g., contribution -> tributary width, convergence -> delta vs braid) lack quantified scales or accessibility considerations.
The DOM/tldraw cross-boundary highlight for spoken reply parts is flagged as net-new and could be under-scoped.
notes: A design exploration that turns the concurrent-cognition swarm into a shape-first, motion-rich, steerable visual language with three altitude levels.
questions: What is the exact visual form of the Pulse glyph (iris, waveform, dot) and has Tim approved it?
Will the weighted slot-scheduler be built in the first cut, or will boost/mute be reduced to mute-only?
Does the River overlay the chat lane or live on a separate board?
How are part->feeder mappings emitted and stored for the 'why did this say that' feature?
What accessibility affordances exist for color/state encodings in the river?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is UX/interaction exploration extending 06 and G7; the file does exactly that, proposing altitudes, motion, steering, and RHM self-reading. It matches.
intention_signals: To make the RHM's concurrent thinking perceivable and steerable for Tim by translating a technical swarm graph into an intuitive, animated terrain that the RHM can also read aloud to explain itself.
novelty: The collapse of steering gesture, resource allocation, and visual shape into one metaphor (widening a tributary = more attention budget), and the treatment of reply provenance as a visual back-lighting interaction rather than a generated explanation.
contribution: A concrete three-altitude visual vocabulary (Pulse/River/Nodes), a set of motion and steering affordances, and a self-explanation interaction pattern that a fused system could keep as the UX specification for the cognition view.
summary_for_embedding: E2-cognition-view-ux.md is a generative UX design exploration for the RHM's concurrent-cognition view, proposing three altitude levels (Pulse, River, Nodes), perceptual encodings for swarm behavior, motion-driven temporal reading, live steering gestures (boost/mute, cast override, reply provenance), and the RHM reading its own cognition as a combined voice-and-canvas interaction.
intention_for_embedding: To define the interaction and visual language that makes a swarm of concurrent role-runs glanceable, steerable, and self-explainable for Tim.
